req.title: Cheque deposit by mobile (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers mobile cheque deposit with a $5,000 manual-verification threshold. I identified four distinct test conditions: (1) a valid cheque below the threshold — EP positive partition confirming 'deposit pending' status; (2) a cheque exactly at the $5,000 boundary — BVA lower boundary of the manual-review zone; (3) a cheque above $5,000 — BVA upper-boundary side confirming decisioning queued for manual review; (4) an image that fails MICR decode — NEG path confirming HTTP 422. A fifth test covers the unauthorised actor path (NEG/DT). Together these cover the positive, boundary, and negative mandates with at least one of each kind. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders, posts to /deposits/mobile-cheque, and makes concise status/body assertions.

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
Cheque amount is $1,000 (well within the $5,000 auto-approval partition)
A valid MICR-decodable cheque image fixture is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=1000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body does NOT contain manual_review flag set to true
deposit_id is present in the response
trace: R-FIN-069: cheque deposit via mobile image
R-FIN-069: deposit pending outcome for amounts below threshold
EP: representative value from valid/auto-approve partition (<$5,000)
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_below_threshold_accepted(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image_b64": valid_cheque_image
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body.get("manual_review") is not True
    assert "deposit_id" in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_threshold_requires_manual_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
Cheque amount is exactly $5,000 (the defined boundary value)
A valid MICR-decodable cheque image fixture is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=5000.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-069: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification
BVA: exact boundary value of $5,000 triggers manual-review partition
R-FIN-069: decisioning queued as expected outcome
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_at_threshold_requires_manual_review(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image_b64": valid_cheque_image
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body["manual_review"] is True
    assert body["decisioning_queued"] is True

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_threshold_decisioning_queued
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
Cheque amount is $5,000.01 (one cent above the boundary, inside manual-review partition)
A valid MICR-decodable cheque image fixture is available
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=5000.01
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-069: cheques above $5,000 require manual verification
BVA: value immediately above boundary ($5,000.01) also triggers manual review
R-FIN-069: decisioning queued as expected outcome
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_above_threshold_decisioning_queued(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000.01,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image_b64": valid_cheque_image
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "deposit_pending"
    assert body["manual_review"] is True
    assert body["decisioning_queued"] is True

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_micr_decode_failure_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
An image fixture that deliberately fails MICR decode is available
Amount is within normal range ($500)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with corrupt/undecodable MICR image and amount=500.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-069: error pathway — 422 when image fails MICR decode
NEG: invalid image input drives error branch
R-FIN-069: system must not silently accept undecipherable cheques
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_micr_decode_failure_returns_422(auth_headers, invalid_micr_image):
    payload = {
        "amount": 500.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image_b64": invalid_micr_image
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "MICR_DECODE_FAILURE"
    assert "deposit_id" not in body

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_unauthorised_returns_401
preconditions: No valid authentication token is provided (missing or expired JWT)
A valid MICR-decodable cheque image fixture is available
Amount is within normal range ($500)
actions: POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image but no Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body error code is 'UNAUTHORIZED' or 'INVALID_TOKEN'
trace: R-FIN-069: negative path — unauthorised actor must be rejected
DT: rule — unauthenticated AND valid image => 401, no deposit

tests.name: test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exceeded_daily_limit_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT in fixture)
Customer daily mobile deposit limit has been exhausted by <seed_max_daily_deposits> helper
A valid MICR-decodable cheque image fixture is available
Cheque amount is $100 (small, so limit is the only blocking factor)
actions: Exhaust the customer's daily deposit limit via <seed_max_daily_deposits>
POST /deposits/mobile-cheque with valid image and amount=100.00
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity or 429 Too Many Requests
No new deposit record is created
trace: R-FIN-069: negative path — exceeded limits must be tested
NEG: customer has consumed their daily allowance; further deposit must fail
R-FIN-069: error pathway for exceeded limits
tests.executable:
def test_mobile_cheque_deposit_exceeded_daily_limit_rejected(auth_headers, valid_cheque_image, customer_id):
    <seed_max_daily_deposits>(customer_id)  # exhaust limit idempotently via test data API
    payload = {
        "amount": 100.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "image_b64": valid_cheque_image
    }
    resp = <api_client>.post("/deposits/mobile-cheque", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (422, 429)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert "deposit_id" not in body
